rulings.Case Title: CALIFORNIA GAMING ASSOCIATION ET AL VS. CALIFORNIA GAMBLING CONTROL COMMISSION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for February 24, 2026, line 1. RESPONDENTS CALIFORNIA GAMBLING CONTROL COMMISSION, CALIFORNIA BUREAU OF GAMBLING CONTROL'S DEMURRER. Respondents and Defendants' demurrer to the second amended petition ("SAP") is sustained without leave to amend as to causes of action brought by David Shindle and overruled as to causes of action brought by California Gaming Association. The court's tentative ruling in its entirety has been sent to counsel via email. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: ISRAEL ROSA VS. THE SFO FORECAST INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of And Unopposed Motion For Entry Of Dismissal Without Prejudice Of Plaintiffs Tenth Cause Of Action Under The Private Attorneys General Act And Dismissal Of Balance Of Action With Prejudice
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for February 24, 2026, line 2. DEFENDANT THE SFO FORECAST INC.'S Unopposed Motion For Entry Of Dismissal Without Prejudice Of Plaintiffs Tenth Cause Of Action Under The Private Attorneys General Act And Dismissal Of Balance Of Action With Prejudice. The motion for entry of dismissal without prejudice is denied without prejudice. Plaintiff has filed an action under PAGA. Labor Code, Sec. 2699, subdivision (s)(2) states, "The superior court shall review and approve any settlement of any civil action filed pursuant to this part. The proposed settlement shall be submitted to the agency at the same time that it is submitted to the court." Although Plaintiff has settled the action individually, this civil action was filed pursuant to PAGA and the court must assess whether it is fair to absent aggrieved employees. Plaintiff's motion provides insufficient information for the court to make that assessment, and does not provide proof that the proposed settlement was submitted to LWDA. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO BANK, NA VS. CRUZ D OUTLAW
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Pursuant To Ccp 437c
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for February 24, 2026, line 6. PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, NA'S Motion For Summary Judgment Pursuant To CCP 437c. Plaintiff Wells Fargo Bank, N.A.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant Cruz D. Outlaw demonstrates there is a triable issue of fact that she incurred the charges appearing on her Wells Fargo credit card after June 23, 2023. (Outlaw Decl., para. 4.) Wells Fargo's objections to this evidence are overruled; Outlaw can attest based on her personal knowledge of what transactions she made. Outlaw does more than state her opinion that fraud occurred; she attests that she stopped using the card entirely after she paid it off: "After June 2023, when my balance was zero, I stopped using the Wells Fargo card. I never made or authorized any further transaction of any kind on that account." (Id.) This is not a hearsay statement or a statement of opinion. Wells Fargo also appears to ask the court to exercise its discretion not to consider Outlaw's declaration. Code of Civil Procedure, sec. 437c, subdivision (e), states that "summary judgment may be denied in the discretion of the court if the only proof of a material fact offered in support of the summary judgment is an affidavit or declaration made by an individual who was the sole witness to that fact . . . ." This section allows the court to deny summary judgment by disregarding an uncorroborated affidavit but does not allow the court to grant summary judgment on that basis. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: HBL HOLDINGS, LLC, VS. FELLOW INDUSTRIES, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Select Portions Of Plaintiff'S Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for February 24, 2026, line 12. PLAINTIFF HBL HOLDINGS, LLC'S Motion To Seal Select Portions Of Plaintiff'S Complaint. The unopposed motion to seal is granted. Defendant has dismissed the complaint and it is therefore not at issue as a basis for adjudication. Accordingly, no findings are required under Rule of Court 2.550. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)
